name: Lemon Curd Tartlettes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, whip the eggs and sugar with an electric mixer attachment until very light and fluffy.
2. Gradually mix in the lemon juice and grated rind. Place over boiling water and cook, whisking until extremely thick, about 5 to 10 minutes. Remove from heat and whisk in butter. Pour into a container, cover with plastic wrap, and chill overnight to stiffen.
3. Lay one sheet of phyllo on a work surface. Brush with clarified butter and sprinkle with sugar. Repeat the process with the second and third sheets. Cut into 3-inch squares.
4. Place 18 tartlette molds upside down on a sheet pan. Lay one square of phyllo over each pan, then place a second one on top of the phyllo to help form the tartlette shells. The square corners will spread out flat on the sheet pan.
5. Bake in this upside-down position, weighted down with another sheet pan at 375°F until golden brown. Let cool slightly but while still warm, remove from molds. Cool completely.
6. Fill each shell with lemon curd using a piping bag with a plain tip.
7. For topping, whip egg whites until stiff but not dry. Gradually add the brown sugar and continue whipping until stiff. Pipe onto lemon curd using a star tip and brown under a blow torch or broiler.
